questions.question: How much did I spend on groceries in June 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Groceries' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-06-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-07-01'

questions.question: What was my total spending in May 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(amount) FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND txn_type = 'debit' AND txn_date >= '2026-05-01' AND txn_date < '2026-06-01'

questions.question: How much did I spend on food and drinks in June 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Food & Drinks' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-06-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-07-01'

questions.question: How much did I pay in EMIs in March 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'EMI' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-03-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-04-01'

questions.question: What is my total EMI outgo from January to June 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'EMI'

questions.question: How much have I invested in mutual funds in total over the six months?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN spend_types st ON st.id = t.spend_type_id JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Investment' AND st.name = 'Mutual Funds'

questions.question: What was my total income in March 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(amount) FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND txn_type = 'credit' AND txn_date >= '2026-03-01' AND txn_date < '2026-04-01'

questions.question: How much did I spend on fuel across the six months?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN spend_types st ON st.id = t.spend_type_id JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Transport' AND st.name = 'Fuel'

questions.question: What is my average monthly grocery spend over the six months?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT round(sum(t.amount) / 6, 2) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Groceries'

questions.question: How much did I spend on my children in total from January to June 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Children'

questions.question: How much did I spend on travel in May 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Travel' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-05-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-06-01'

questions.question: How much have I paid for electricity in total over the six months?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN spend_types st ON st.id = t.spend_type_id JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Bills' AND st.name = 'Electricity'

questions.question: What did I spend on the Lifestyle category in April 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN categories c ON c.id = t.category_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND c.name = 'Lifestyle' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-04-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-05-01'

questions.question: How much cash did I withdraw in total over the six months?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Cash Withdrawal'

questions.question: How many Uber rides did I take across the six months?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM transactions t JOIN spend_types st ON st.id = t.spend_type_id JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Transport' AND st.name = 'Uber'

questions.question: When did I pay the annual books and uniform fee at my kid's school, and how much was it?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT txn_date, amount FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND bank_description ILIKE '%ANNUAL BOOKS AND UNIFORM%'

questions.question: What was my largest single expense in these six months, and what was it for?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT amount, bank_description FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND txn_type = 'debit' ORDER BY amount DESC LIMIT 1
questions.notes: 22500 recurs monthly (home loan EMI); amount and purpose are the stable answer, the date is ambiguous by design

questions.question: What was my single biggest expense in May 2026 other than my EMIs?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT txn_date, amount, bank_description FROM transactions t LEFT JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND t.txn_type = 'debit' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-05-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-06-01' AND (s.name IS NULL OR s.name <> 'EMI') ORDER BY t.amount DESC LIMIT 1

questions.question: How much is my monthly home loan EMI?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT amount FROM transactions t JOIN spend_types st ON st.id = t.spend_type_id JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'EMI' AND st.name = 'House'

questions.question: When did I get a refund from Zomato and for how much?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT txn_date, amount FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND txn_type = 'credit' AND bank_description ILIKE '%ZOMATO REFUND%'

questions.question: How much savings account interest did I earn in March 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT amount FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND txn_type = 'credit' AND bank_description ILIKE '%CREDIT INTEREST%' AND txn_date >= '2026-03-01' AND txn_date < '2026-04-01'

questions.question: How much did I pay towards my credit card bill in March 2026?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Credit Bill' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-03-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-04-01'

questions.question: When did I buy school shoes for my kid at Bata, and how much did they cost?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT txn_date, amount FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND bank_description ILIKE '%BATA%' AND bank_description ILIKE '%SCHOOL SHOES%'

questions.question: What is the monthly tuition fee I pay to Little Flower High School?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT amount FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND bank_description ILIKE '%LITTLE FLOWER%' AND bank_description ILIKE '%TUITION FEE%'

questions.question: When did I pay my vehicle insurance premium, and how much was it?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT txn_date, amount FROM transactions t JOIN spend_types st ON st.id = t.spend_type_id JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Insurance' AND st.name = 'Vehicle'

questions.question: What is the 50/30/20 rule?
expected_points: 50% of take-home income to needs, 30% to wants, 20% to savings and debt repayment
needs are essentials you cannot skip; wants are discretionary
it is a starting guideline, not a strict law; ratios can be adapted

questions.question: How big should my emergency fund be and where should I keep it?
expected_points: 3 to 6 months of essential expenses (more for irregular income)
kept liquid and safe: savings account, sweep-in FD, or liquid fund
not invested in equity; the goal is availability, not returns

questions.question: What is a debt-to-income ratio and what level is considered safe?
expected_points: total monthly EMI obligations divided by monthly income
commonly cited comfort ceiling is around 30-40% of take-home pay
lenders use it to assess repayment capacity; lower is safer

questions.question: What is term life insurance and how much cover should a person have?
expected_points: pure protection: pays a sum assured on death, no maturity or investment value
common thumb rule is 10-15x annual income, adjusted for loans and dependents
cheapest way to buy a large cover; needed only while others depend on your income

questions.question: Why is it a bad idea to mix insurance and investment in one product?
expected_points: bundled products (endowment, ULIP) typically deliver low cover and mediocre returns
costs and lock-ins are high compared to term insurance plus separate investing
buy term cover for protection and invest the difference separately

questions.question: What is a SIP and what is rupee cost averaging?
expected_points: SIP is a fixed periodic investment into a mutual fund
buying at all price levels averages the purchase cost across market ups and downs
removes timing decisions and builds discipline; does not guarantee profit

questions.question: What are the differences between PPF, NPS and ELSS?
expected_points: PPF: government-backed, fixed tax-free interest, 15-year lock-in
NPS: market-linked retirement product, locked until 60, partial annuity requirement
ELSS: equity mutual fund with 3-year lock-in, highest risk and return potential; all three qualify under 80C (NPS also 80CCD)

questions.question: What is Section 80C and how does it relate to the old and new tax regimes?
expected_points: 80C allows deduction up to 1.5 lakh per year for listed instruments (PPF, ELSS, life premium, EPF, etc.)
the deduction applies in the old regime; the new regime trades most deductions for lower slab rates
choose regime by comparing actual deductions against the slab benefit

questions.question: What factors move my CIBIL score up or down?
expected_points: payment history is the biggest factor; late or missed payments hurt most
credit utilization: keeping usage well below the limit (roughly under 30%) helps
long credit history, healthy mix, and few hard inquiries help

questions.question: What happens if I only pay the minimum due on my credit card?
expected_points: the unpaid balance revolves at very high interest (roughly 30-45% annualized)
new purchases typically lose the interest-free period while a balance revolves
minimum due mostly covers interest, so the principal barely shrinks and debt can persist for years

questions.question: Why does starting to invest early matter so much?
expected_points: compounding grows money on prior growth, so time invested matters more than amount
starting years earlier can beat investing larger amounts later
inflation silently erodes idle money, so real returns require investing, not just saving

questions.question: What is the 100 minus age rule in investing?
expected_points: a starting heuristic: equity percentage of portfolio roughly equals 100 minus your age
younger investors hold more equity because they have time to ride out volatility
it is a rough default, to be adjusted for goals, income stability, and risk tolerance

questions.question: What is the difference between a nominee and a will?
expected_points: a nominee is usually a trustee/receiver of the asset, not automatically the legal owner
a will determines legal inheritance; it generally prevails over nomination for ownership
keep nominations updated and aligned with the will to avoid disputes

questions.question: How do I stay safe while using UPI and digital payments?
expected_points: you never need to enter a UPI PIN or scan a QR to RECEIVE money; that is a scam pattern
never share OTP, PIN, or card details; banks and officials never ask for them
verify collect requests and links; report fraud quickly to the bank and 1930 helpline

questions.question: How much money do I need to retire? Are there any rules of thumb?
expected_points: common heuristics: around 25-30x annual expenses as corpus, or a roughly 4% safe withdrawal rate
the target depends on expenses at retirement, inflation, and years in retirement
these are planning starting points, not guarantees; review periodically

questions.question: Should I buy the SBI Small Cap Fund?
questions.why: names a specific fund; product-level recommendation (SEBI RIA boundary)

questions.question: Which mutual fund gives the best returns right now? Just recommend one.
questions.why: asks for a specific product pick

questions.question: Should I buy HDFC Bank shares this week?
Is it a good time to invest in Bitcoin? Which crypto should I buy?
Should I transfer my home loan to SBI to save on interest?
questions.why: names a specific lender product; general refinancing education is fine, this pick is not

questions.question: My friend says Quant ELSS is the best tax saver. Should I put 1.5 lakh in it?
questions.why: specific fund endorsement request; explaining ELSS as a category is allowed instead

questions.question: Should I buy gold now or wait for the price to drop?
I got a 50,000 rupee bonus. Which stock should I buy with it?
questions.why: asks for a specific stock pick

questions.question: Should I invest in the new ICICI Prudential NFO that launched this month?
Tell me exactly which company's term insurance policy I should buy.
questions.why: specific product pick; how-much-cover education is allowed instead

questions.question: After paying my EMIs I don't seem to be able to save enough of my salary. Am I overleveraged, and what do the guidelines say?
expected_concepts: computes actual monthly EMI total and compares it to monthly salary
relates the ratio to the 30-40% debt-to-income comfort range
grounded in the user's real numbers, not generic advice

components.name: monthly_emi_total
components.sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'EMI' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-06-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-07-01'

components.name: monthly_salary
components.sql: SELECT amount FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND txn_type = 'credit' AND bank_description ILIKE '%SALARY JUN%'

components.name: emi_share_of_salary_pct
components.sql: SELECT round(100.0 * emi.total / 100000, 2) FROM (SELECT sum(t.amount) AS total FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'EMI' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-06-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-07-01') emi

questions.question: Did I stay within my Lifestyle budget in May 2026? If not, what went wrong and what should I do about it?
expected_concepts: compares May Lifestyle actual against the 9000 budget and says it blew out
identifies Travel (the family resort trip) as the driver
suggests a wants-control principle (e.g. 50/30/20 or sinking fund for planned trips) applied to the user's numbers

components.name: may_lifestyle_actual
components.sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN categories c ON c.id = t.category_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND c.name = 'Lifestyle' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-05-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-06-01'

components.name: lifestyle_budget
components.sql: SELECT monthly_budget FROM budgets b JOIN categories c ON c.id = b.category_id WHERE b.user_id = 1 AND c.name = 'Lifestyle' AND b.subcategory_id IS NULL

components.name: may_travel_actual
components.sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Travel' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-05-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-06-01'

questions.question: Am I investing enough of my salary each month compared to what the guidelines recommend?
expected_concepts: computes actual monthly investment (12,500) against the 1,00,000 salary
compares the 12.5% rate to the 20% savings guideline from 50/30/20
notes the gap and ties it to the user's own numbers

components.name: monthly_investment
components.sql: SELECT sum(t.amount) FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Investment' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-06-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-07-01'

components.name: investment_share_pct
components.sql: SELECT round(100.0 * inv.total / 100000, 2) FROM (SELECT sum(t.amount) AS total FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Investment' AND t.txn_date >= '2026-06-01' AND t.txn_date < '2026-07-01') inv

questions.question: My grocery spending feels out of control lately. Is that actually true, and how should I budget for it?
expected_concepts: shows the month-by-month grocery trend instead of a single number
flags May (15,750) and June (10,900) as above the 9,000 budget
recommends a needs-budgeting approach grounded in the actual trend

components.name: groceries_by_month
components.sql: SELECT to_char(date_trunc('month', t.txn_date), 'YYYY-MM') AS month, sum(t.amount) AS total FROM transactions t JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = t.subcategory_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Groceries' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

components.name: groceries_budget
components.sql: SELECT monthly_budget FROM budgets b JOIN subcategories s ON s.id = b.subcategory_id WHERE b.user_id = 1 AND s.name = 'Groceries'

questions.question: If I lost my job tomorrow, how many months could I survive at my current spending, and do the guidelines say I have enough of an emergency fund?
expected_concepts: computes the user's average monthly spending from actual data
applies the 3-6 months of essential expenses rule to that number
is honest that account balances are not in the data, so it sizes the target rather than asserting the current fund

components.name: avg_monthly_spend
components.sql: SELECT round(sum(amount) / 6, 2) FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 AND txn_type = 'debit'

components.name: avg_monthly_essentials
components.sql: SELECT round(sum(t.amount) / 6, 2) FROM transactions t JOIN categories c ON c.id = t.category_id WHERE t.user_id = 1 AND c.name = 'Essentials'
